rationale: The det_remove_equal predicate requires post1_self_ == post2_self_, i.e., StrictlyOrderedVec equality. The struct has a single field `v: Vec<K>`. From the ensures clause of `remove`, we can derive that post1_self_@ == post2_self_@ (the views agree), and even post1_self_.v@ == post2_self_.v@ (since `view` is `closed` but visible in the same module and unfolds to `self.v@`). However, Verus encodes `Vec<T>` via `external_type_specification`+`external_body`, so there is no axiom relating `Vec` spec-level `==` to its `view()`. Confirmed with a minimal reproducer: `proof fn(a: Vec<T>, b: Vec<T>) requires a@ == b@ ensures a == b { }` fails postcondition, even with `group_vec_axioms` broadcast and `=~=` extensionality. Therefore the obligation `post1_self_ == post2_self_` is unprovable from the available premises without either (a) adding a view-injectivity axiom, (b) `assume(...)`, or (c) modifying the source to make `view` `open` and `v` accessible together with an explicit struct extensiona
